name: AI Translation Engine
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Advanced translation intelligence — GPT-4o for nuanced literary and marketing translations requiring cultural adaptation, idiom handling, and tone preservation; GPT-4o-mini for high-volume routine translations (product descriptions, UI strings, support articles); few-shot prompting with domain glossaries for terminology consistency across documents

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput, real-time multilingual content pipeline

services.name: Azure AI Translator
services.purpose: Neural machine translation backbone — document translation (PDF, DOCX, PPTX) with layout preservation, custom translator models trained on domain-specific parallel corpora (legal, medical, technical), glossary enforcement for brand terms and product names, language detection, and transliteration for non-Latin scripts

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Translation memory and glossary store — translation memory database for fuzzy matching against previously translated segments (TM leverage reduces costs by 30-60%), multilingual glossaries with term variants and context rules, translation request tracking, user preferences, quality scores, and project metadata with low-latency reads for real-time translation suggestions

services.name: Azure CDN
services.purpose: Translated content delivery — cached translated web content, localized media assets (images with embedded text, videos with subtitles), static translated documents, and localized API responses served from edge locations nearest to end users for sub-50ms delivery globally

enterprise.note: ~5TB transfer, global edge delivery, custom rules

services.name: Azure Blob Storage
services.purpose: Document and asset storage — source documents awaiting translation, translated output files with preserved formatting, parallel corpora for custom translator training, translation memory export/import files (TMX/XLIFF), and localized media assets

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Translator API keys, OpenAI API keys, Cosmos DB connection strings, CDN tokens, third-party translation vendor API credentials, and webhook signing secrets

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Translation platform observability — translation latency per language pair, quality score distribution (BLEU/COMET), TM leverage ratios, glossary hit rates, character consumption tracking, error rates by document type, and cost-per-word analytics

optimization_tips: Use Azure AI Translator for high-volume routine translations (product listings, UI strings) and reserve Azure OpenAI for culturally nuanced content (marketing copy, legal documents) — Translator costs ~$10/M characters vs GPT-4o at ~$15/M tokens, 5-10x cheaper for straightforward text

Build and maintain translation memory in Cosmos DB — fuzzy matching against previously translated segments provides 30-60% TM leverage, directly reducing the volume of text that requires fresh AI translation

Train custom Azure Translator models on domain-specific parallel corpora (minimum 10K sentence pairs) — custom models outperform generic models for specialized domains and reduce the need for expensive GPT-4o post-editing

Use GPT-4o-mini for translation quality estimation (QE) scoring instead of human review for routine content — automated QE at $0.15/1M tokens identifies segments needing human review, reducing human QA costs by 70%
Implement CDN caching for translated web content with locale-based cache keys — translated pages rarely change, and edge caching eliminates re-translation and reduces origin requests by 90%

Batch document translations during off-peak hours using Azure Translator async document translation API — batch processing is more efficient than real-time per-page translation and reduces API call overhead
